title: The Big Party
learningGoal: The learner will decode narrative text utilizing the Present Perfect tense, identifying how 'have/has + V3' structures communicate recently completed actions that establish a state of current readiness.
instructions: Read the story about a family preparing for a big party. Notice how they use 'have' and 'has' to talk about the jobs they recently finished. Pay close attention to how the action verbs change into their 3rd form (V3)!

contentItems.text: Look closely! Why do some family members say 'I have', while others say 'He has'?

readingText: Today is Amit's birthday. His family is preparing for a big party. His mother is in the kitchen. She is smiling. 'I have baked the cake,' she says. 'And I have cooked the food.' Amit's father walks into the room. He says, 'I have cleaned the house. It looks beautiful.' Amit's sister, Priya, is holding her phone. 'I have called all the guests. They will arrive soon,' she says. Amit is very excited. He looks around the house. The family has done so much work. Someone knocks on the door. 'The pizza delivery man has arrived!' Amit shouts. Suddenly, Amit's dog, Bruno, runs into the room with mud on his paws. 'Oh no!' his father says. 'Bruno has ruined the floor! I must clean it again.' Everything is ready, but first, they must stop the dog.
textInTargetLanguage: Today is Amit's birthday. His family is preparing for a big party. His mother is in the kitchen. She is smiling. 'I have baked the cake,' she says. 'And I have cooked the food.' Amit's father walks into the room. He says, 'I have cleaned the house. It looks beautiful.' Amit's sister, Priya, is holding her phone. 'I have called all the guests. They will arrive soon,' she says. Amit is very excited. He looks around the house. The family has done so much work. Someone knocks on the door. 'The pizza delivery man has arrived!' Amit shouts. Suddenly, Amit's dog, Bruno, runs into the room with mud on his paws. 'Oh no!' his father says. 'Bruno has ruined the floor! I must clean it again.' Everything is ready, but first, they must stop the dog.
baseLanguageSupportText: आज अमित का जन्मदिन है। उनका परिवार एक बड़ी पार्टी की तैयारी कर रहा है। उसकी माँ रसोई में है। वह मुस्कुरा रही है। 'मैंने केक बेक कर लिया है,' वह कहती है। 'और मैंने खाना पका लिया है।' अमित के पिता कमरे में आते हैं। वह कहते हैं, 'मैंने घर साफ कर दिया है। यह सुंदर लग रहा है।' अमित की बहन, प्रिया, अपना फोन पकड़े हुए है। 'मैंने सभी मेहमानों को बुला (कॉल कर) लिया है। वे जल्द ही पहुंचेंगे,' वह कहती है। अमित बहुत उत्साहित है। वह घर के चारों ओर देखता है। परिवार ने बहुत काम किया है। कोई दरवाज़ा खटखटाता है। 'पिज्ज़ा डिलीवरी करने वाला आदमी आ गया है!' अमित चिल्लाता है। अचानक, अमित का कुत्ता, ब्रूनो, अपने पंजों पर कीचड़ के साथ कमरे में दौड़ता है। 'अरे नहीं!' उसके पिता कहते हैं। 'ब्रूनो ने फर्श खराब कर दिया है! मुझे इसे फिर से साफ करना होगा।' सब कुछ तैयार है, लेकिन पहले उन्हें कुत्ते को रोकना होगा।

exampleSentence.baseTranslation: मैंने केक बेक कर लिया है।
मैंने घर साफ कर दिया है।

glossary.baseTranslation: आ गया है / पहुँच गया है
बर्बाद कर दिया है / खराब कर दिया है

exampleSentence.baseTranslation: ब्रूनो ने फर्श खराब कर दिया है।

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: I have called all the guests.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: आई हैव कॉल्ड ऑल द गेस्ट्स।
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: मैंने सभी मेहमानों को कॉल कर लिया है।

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: The family has done so much work.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: द फैमिली हैज़ डन सो मच वर्क।
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: परिवार ने बहुत काम किया है।
ब्रूनो ने फर्श खराब कर दिया है!

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: What has the mother done in the kitchen?
Fill in the blank: The father says, 'I _____ cleaned the house.'
What has Priya done on her phone?
Look exactly at the text. Does Amit say 'The pizza man have arrived' or 'The pizza man has arrived'?
Why does the pizza man take 'has' instead of 'have'?
options: Because he is a single person (He/She/It rule)

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: What terrible thing has Bruno the dog done?
Which phrase proves that the family finished an action (Verb 3 of 'Do')?
True or False: The text uses 'I have cooked', ending the verb in '-ed'. That means 'cooked' is the V3 firm.

correctAnswers.q5: Because he is a single person (He/She/It rule)

expectedSummaryPoints: The learner correlates the Present Perfect (Have/Has + V3) to task completion.
The learner recognizes the structural shift between 1st person agreement ('I have cleaned') and 3rd person agreement ('Bruno has ruined').

feedbackRules.onPass: Magnificent! You perfectly tracked who had completed which job for the party. You successfully noticed the difference between 'I HAVE' and 'He HAS'!
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep trying! Remember, 'I' always takes 'Have'. Third person singular nouns (like 'The pizza man' or 'The dog' or 'The family') always take 'Has'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Pay close attention to question 7. The V3 form of 'Do' is 'Done'! (Do -> Did -> Done). That's why the text says 'The family has done'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Today is Amit's birthday. His family is preparing for a big party... (full transcript provided in text)

hintText: Read the text carefully to double-check who gets 'Have' and who gets 'Has'!
commonMistakes: Thinking 'The pizza man' takes 'Have' because they are confused about third-person singular rules.
